items.title: Trump’s 10% tariff blow that could work in India’s favour
items.summary: The US Section 301 tariff has escalated cost challenges for Indian exporters, but it simultaneously provides a unique advantage. Competing nations are subjected to higher tariffs, allowing Indian goods to be more attractive. This favorable situation highlights the US's recognition of India's efforts against forced labor. By leveraging government assistance and adopting proactive measures, Indian exporters can capitalize on trade shifts and continue their growth trajectory.

items.title: AAIB flags air traffic controllers' procedural lapses in airprox incident; suggests measures to AAI
items.summary: An airprox incident involving two Boeing 777 aircraft occurred in Indian airspace on March 24, 2024. The probe agency recommended Airports Authority of India improve controller watch briefings. Procedural lapses and over-reliance on automated systems contributed to the serious incident. The agency also stressed sensitizing controllers about safety-critical Standard Operating Procedures. Strengthening on-the-job training supervision protocols was also advised by the bureau.

Aiming to enhance domestic production and minimize import reliance, the scheme is poised to attract considerable investments and generate new employment opportunities.

items.title: Regulator warns insurers against deep discounts on fire risk cover
items.summary: The insurance regulator has flagged aggressive underpricing in fire insurance, with discounts of up to 99% on large industrial risks, warning it could hurt insurers’ financial health and underwriting discipline. Fire insurance premiums fell sharply in Q1 FY27 to ₹8,087 crore from ₹11,206 crore a year ago, after strong growth in FY26.

items.title: Global demand puts India's two-wheeler exports back in top gear
items.summary: Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor reported strong international sales in the June quarter. This marks a significant turnaround for India's two-wheeler export market. Total motorcycle and scooter exports rose nearly thirty-seven percent year-on-year. Demand across Africa, Latin America, and Asia is powering this export growth. The domestic market also shows resilience, aided by recent GST reductions.

items.title: Clean power capacity all set to cross 300 GW: Pralhad Joshi
items.summary: India is set to cross 300 GW of non-fossil fuel power capacity soon, with current capacity at 297 GW, accounting for over half of total installed power. The government targets 500 GW non-fossil capacity by 2030 as part of its clean energy push. The PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana is expected to cover over 5 million households in the coming weeks, boosting rooftop solar adoption.

items.title: B9 board, investors set out to find buyers for founder Ankur Jain's stake
items.summary: Bira 91 is currently exploring options for selling founder Ankur Jain's stake, with its board and investors actively pursuing potential buyers. They have reached out to various family offices and corporate entities to facilitate the sale. To resume operations, the company estimates an infusion of approximately ₹500 crore will be necessary.

items.title: ASG shareholder approaches NCLT against co-founder, alleges oppression

items.title: Pulses import bill shrinks as global prices fall, local production rises
items.summary: India's import bill for key pulses significantly decreased in FY26 due to robust domestic production. Lower global prices and import curbs also contributed to this substantial reduction. Exports of certain pulses, like Kabuli chana, saw a notable increase during the same period. This shift reflects India's growing self-sufficiency and improved market position for these commodities. Domestic policies and strong harvests have bolstered India's pulse sector performance.

This move will help increase India's exports without impacting small retailers.

"As soon as tomorrow, USTR will release its final responsive action on its Section 301 investigation of our top 60 trading partners' failure to address international trade in forced goods at their borders," he said.

items.title: ET Graphics: India is winning the battle against hunger, but not food costs
items.summary: India has achieved a 42% decline in undernourished people over two decades. The number of Indians unable to afford a healthy diet also fell significantly. This progress outpaces global trends in tackling hunger and malnutrition. However, the cost of a healthy diet has risen by over a third. These findings are based on a new report by the UN's Food and Agriculture Organization.

items.title: JSW Infrastructure eyes 127 MT cargo in FY27 even as Fujairah terminal disruption continues
items.summary: JSW Infrastructure anticipates handling 127 million tonnes of cargo this fiscal year. Operations at its Fujairah liquid terminal are gradually returning to normal. Eight storage tanks will be operational by late July or early August. The company is also proceeding with significant domestic expansion plans. Profit for the June quarter saw a slight decrease compared to last year.

Foreign exchange reserves are comfortable, covering over ten months of imports.

items.title: External sectors steady despite geopolitical uncertainties: RBI

Foreign exchange reserves provide ample cover for imports and external debt. Exports and imports saw strong growth in the first quarter of this fiscal year. Bilateral trade agreements will further enhance India's external trade momentum.
